title: sedum angustum
intro: Sedum angustum, also known as the Narrow-leaved Stonecrop, is a species of succulent plant in the family Crassulaceae, characterized by its narrow leaves and ability to store water in its stems. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immune-boosting effects. Sedum angustum can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been traditionally used in folk medicine to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of various diseases.
